segments.title: Rome: Total War as a history gateway
segments.summary: A short opening note explains why the original Rome: Total War mattered to Dr. Clarke: it connected childhood play with history, battles, ships, and naval fights.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke describes the original Rome: Total War as the game that defined his childhood interest in historical gaming. While other players were absorbed in Halo, he was drawn to Rome's combination of history and battles. Ships and naval fights were part of that appeal, linking the game to his wider interest in military and naval history. The naval component was limited, however: the player received ships and naval engagements without directly controlling the ships in battle.

evidence.note: The transcript identifies the original game and describes it as Dr. Clarke's childhood favorite.
The transcript links the appeal to history, battles, ships, and naval fights.
The transcript closes the clip by anticipating the game positively.
